other_observers: Tony Leukering and about a dozen birders from Wyoming for whom I was leading a fieldtrip.; Bill Schmoker and Rachel Hopper on Nov 6, 2004 at Terry Lake, Larimer County.
description: Very large, dark-backed gull. Wings and mantle were a very dark charcoal gray intermixed with brown first-year feathers; tertials had broad white tips; primary tips appeared all dark; underparts were mostly white except showing some dark streaking on the sides and chest; head was also mostly white except for dark streaking on the hindneck and around eyes; the bill was pinkish-yellow with a distinctly demarcated black tip. This individual was larger than Herring Gulls in close proximity.Behaviors: It rested on the water the entire time.Call: None heard.Plumage: second winter
